same_day_appointments.notes: A limited number of additional appointments are released at most offices daily and made available throughout the day; these can be scheduled online.

exceptions: business needs (parental authorization, residency/address change, etc.)

faq_operational_rules.no_appointment_option: If arriving without an appointment, customer may use a self-service kiosk to schedule if available that day, or schedule another day/location.
faq_operational_rules.reschedule_process: Reschedule via scheduler; existing appointment is automatically cancelled upon confirmation of a new appointment.
faq_operational_rules.confirmation_process: Provided at booking; can be obtained later by logging back into scheduler.

notifications.recommendation: Adding a valid email is best for receiving pertinent information.

faq_operational_rules.no_local_office_listed_reason: If local office is not listed, there are no available appointments; select next closest office.
faq_operational_rules.online_eligibility_check: Scheduler may advise if eligible to complete service online via Texas.gov.

notes: Automated menu routes calls to the right team
Wait times can exceed 20 minutes during peak time due to high call volume
Tip: Call 7:00 am–8:00 am for non-peak time

when_user_requests_contact_link: Optionally provide phone number and hours

Lost or Stolen Driver License/ID Card
Reporting an Error on a DL/ID
Failure to Appear / Failure to Pay (FTA/FTP)
License and Permit Specialist Responsibilities / DL Division FAQs
Request Knowledge Tests by Driver Education Schools

metadata.title: TX_DPS_DL_faq_sections_links_v1

availability_notes: Contribution opportunities and program information are made available in all driver license offices.
Donations may also be made online during driver license and identification card transactions.
Contribution opportunities can only be made when conducting a driver license or identification card transaction and may not be available for all transactions.

programs.name: Donate Life Texas
programs.type: Organ, eye, and tissue donor registry (after-death donation registration via DPS)
programs.who_can_participate: Applicants/renewals for Texas DL, ID, or CDL during transaction
programs.key_note: Registration through DPS covers donation after death only (not living donation).

programs.name: Living Organ Donation (Information)
programs.type: Information on living donation (kidney or part of liver)
programs.eligibility_high_level: Potential living donor must be 18+ and in good physical and mental health; screening required.

programs.name: Glenda Dawson Donate Life Texas Donor Registry
programs.type: Donation-funded registry named in honor of Glenda Dawson

programs.name: Blindness Education Screening and Treatment Program (BEST)
programs.type: Free vision screenings + financial assistance for medically urgent eye treatment (with ophthalmologist referral)
programs.who_can_donate: Applicants/renewals for Texas DL or ID during transaction

programs.name: Texas Veterans Assistance Fund
programs.type: Voluntary contribution fund supporting veterans in need
programs.who_can_donate: Applicants/renewals for Texas DL or ID during transaction
assists_with_examples: Critical home repairs and accessibility improvements for service-connected disabled
Rehabilitation support for justice-involved veterans through specialized courts

programs.name: Survivors of Sexual Assault (Evidence Testing Support)
programs.type: Voluntary contributions supporting testing of collected evidence for sexual assault/sex offenses
programs.administered_by: Criminal Justice Division of the Office of the Governor (grant program)

programs.name: Texas Bone Marrow Donation & Transplant
programs.type: Bone marrow donor recruitment program (education + recruitment)

programs.name: Identification Fee Exemption Fund
programs.type: Assists in payment of DL/ID fees for foster and homeless children/youths and victims/children of family or dating violence

metadata.title: TX_DPS_DL_programs_donations_facts_v1

renewal_window.notes: CDL can only be renewed up to 1 year before expiration; otherwise renewals are allowed up to 2 years before/after expiration.

dl_eligibility_requirements: Class C/M/CM OR CDL without Hazmat endorsement (Hazmat cannot renew online)
Expires within 2 years OR expired less than 2 years
No significant vision/physical/mental health impact since last renewal
CDL only: valid medical certificate on file
U.S. citizen with Social Security number on record
Special case: will be 18 within 30 days and holds provisional license
id_eligibility_requirements: Renewed ID in person last time
Expires within 2 years OR expired less than 2 years
U.S. citizen with Social Security number on record
what_you_need: Most recently issued DL/ID and audit number
Printer or email address for temporary license and receipt (not available by phone)

mail_in.notes: If invited to renew by mail, follow instructions on mailed form; online/phone also available if eligible.

in_person.when_required: If not eligible for online, telephone, or mail renewal
in_person.provisional_under_18_extra: Must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian.

proof_requirements: U.S. citizenship OR evidence of lawful presence

ssn_verification.method: Verified electronically with the U.S. government
ssn_verification.failure_outcome: If SSN cannot be verified, the ID card cannot be issued or renewed

preparation_tool.name: REAL ID Document Check Application
preparation_tool.purpose: Generates a customized checklist of documents to bring for the office visit

apply_in_person_steps: Complete the identification card application before arriving (also available at offices)
Make an appointment at a driver license office
Present application + required proof documents (lawful presence, residency, identity)

eligibility_restriction.rule_text: If applicant holds a driver license, they are no longer eligible to apply for an ID card unless they surrender the driver license at the time of application

eligibility: Currently holds a REAL ID compliant Texas DL or CDL
process: Create/sign in to TxT account at Texas.gov
Select 'Surrender DL for ID' option and submit verification info

metadata.title: TX_DPS_ID_apply_facts_v1

senior_65_plus_online_id_option.purpose: Voluntarily surrender driver license and apply for a Texas identification card (ID) without visiting an office
eligibility: Holds a REAL ID compliant Texas driver license or commercial driver license
how_to: Create or sign in to a Texas-by-Texas (TxT) account (Texas.gov)
Select 'Surrender DL for ID' and submit required verification information (identity, citizenship, age)

office_visit_steps: Schedule an appointment at local driver license office
Complete driver license application (online or at office)
Specialist reviews application/medical history for additional testing needs
Provide required documentation (application; citizenship/lawful presence as applicable; identity proof)

possible_additional_requirements_if_conc: Additional medical information from a doctor may be requested
testing_policy.reference: Texas Medical Evaluation Process for Driver Licensing page is linked from the senior drivers page

linked_resources: Texas Department of State Health Services (DSHS)
metadata.title: TX_DPS_senior_drivers_65_plus_and_79_plus_facts_v1
